refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end under agreed network conditions to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly specify the latency metric (p95 ≤ 150 ms) and the end-to-end scope, clarifying the performance requirement critical for safety.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency of teleoperation commands and feedback under defined network conditions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link performance to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide timely telemetry and tasking data exchange to maintain control responsiveness.

candidate_dps: DP1: Optimize data encoding and compression to reduce payload size.
DP2: Use dedicated low-latency network protocols and QoS prioritization.
DP3: Implement edge processing to minimize data round-trip times.
DP4: Design telemetry and tasking message batching and scheduling.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP2: Use dedicated low-latency network protocols and QoS prioritization.

potential_sources: Telemetry and Tasking interfaces tightly coupled with network link performance.
Dependency on Safety Services for latency monitoring and alerts.
coupling_check.notes: DP2 logically isolates latency control to network protocol and QoS layers, minimizing cross-domain coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions are within agreed parameters.
Risk of network congestion or failure impacting latency.
Assumes Safety Services can monitor and react to latency breaches.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider adding fallback mechanisms or adaptive QoS to handle network variability.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for implementing teleoperation communication protocols, latency optimization, and real-time data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Bi-directional telemetry data exchange between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Tasking commands received from NGHE Solution or Site Manager routed to Teleoperation.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network interface supporting low-latency communication.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Latency breach alerts sent to Safety Services for safety monitoring.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of latency-related functions; low diffusion as latency logic is centralized.
Vehicle module handles telemetry but latency control is less direct.
Monitors alerts but does not implement latency control.
System-level coordination but no direct latency implementation.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Latency control logic and communication protocol optimization are best contained within the Teleoperation module to minimize diffusion and enable focused ownership.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle telemetry and Safety Services alerts.
Risk of latency logic duplication if other modules implement partial latency controls.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts and ownership boundaries to prevent diffusion and duplication.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (low-latency network protocols and QoS) directly addresses the refined FR of latency ≤ 150 ms with clear assumptions and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Latency logic diffusion risk mitigated by centralizing in Teleoperation module.
Interface complexity with Vehicle and Safety Services noted but manageable.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as the best IM boundary for latency control.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified end-to-end via telemetry and network monitoring tools within Teleoperation module scope.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency control to Teleoperation module with defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface definitions between Teleoperation and Vehicle to ensure latency data paths are optimized.
Establish monitoring and alert protocols with Safety Services for latency breaches.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, interface definitions and fallback strategies require refinement to mitigate risks and improve robustness.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the teleoperation data path.
FR2: Ensure reliable network link performance to support latency requirements.
FR3: Provide timely telemetry and tasking data exchange to maintain control responsiveness.

revise.why: Clarify and strengthen interface contracts and ownership boundaries to reduce diffusion and duplication risks.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Safety Services can reliably monitor latency alerts.
open_risks: Network congestion or failure impacting latency.
Potential latency logic duplication across modules.
evidence_needed: Latency performance test results under representative network conditions.
Interface compliance verification between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
